proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009` equal to 0.

Cell lines with `feature_027` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027` equal to 0.

analyses.result_summary: `feature_009` == 1: mean dependency_BRAF was -0.8047 in the subgroup (n=5574) vs -0.2495 in all other lines; effect=-0.5552, p=<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with both `feature_011` equal to `colorectal` and `feature_035` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than expected from the separate effects of those two predicates.

Cell lines with both `feature_011` equal to `colorectal` and `feature_028` in the top quartile (>= 0.819) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than expected from the separate effects of those two predicates.
Among the `feature_011` equal to `colorectal` pattern, `feature_020` equal to 1 suppresses `dependency_KIF18A`, making scores less negative than expected under additivity.

Cell lines with `feature_011` equal to `colorectal`, `feature_035` equal to 1, `feature_028` in the top quartile (>= 0.819), and `feature_020` equal to 0 have especially negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores compared with all other cell lines.

In a systematic pairwise screen, cell lines with both `feature_011` equal to `brain` and `feature_027` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than expected under additivity.
In a systematic pairwise screen, cell lines with both `feature_011` equal to `colorectal` and `feature_035` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than expected under additivity.

In a systematic pairwise screen, cell lines with both `feature_011` equal to `brain` and `feature_036` in the top quartile (>= 0.165) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than expected under additivity.
In a systematic pairwise screen, cell lines with both `feature_020` equal to 1 and `feature_021` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than expected under additivity.
In a systematic pairwise screen, cell lines with both `feature_011` equal to `breast` and `feature_030` equal to 1 have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than expected under additivity.

In a systematic pairwise screen, cell lines with both `feature_011` equal to `ovarian` and `feature_002` in the bottom quartile (<= 2.963) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than expected under additivity.

Final KIF18A subgroup: cell lines with `feature_011` equal to `colorectal`, `feature_035` equal to 1, `feature_028` in the top quartile (>= 0.819), and `feature_020` equal to 0 have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Final TMED10 subgroup: cell lines with `feature_016` in the top quartile (>= 0.964) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than all other cell lines.

Final POLR2A subgroup: cell lines with `feature_016` in the top quartile (>= 0.964) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than all other cell lines.

Final RPL11 subgroup: cell lines with `feature_018` equal to 1, `feature_003` in the top quartile (>= 0.428), and `feature_016` in the top quartile (>= 0.964) have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t test, subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011` == `colorectal`, `feature_035` == 1, `feature_028` in the top quartile (>= 0.819), and `feature_020` == 0: mean dependency_KIF18A was -1.2990 in the subgroup (n=2571) vs -0.2530 in all other lines; effect=-1.0460, p=<1e-300.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t test, subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016` in the top quartile (>= 0.964): mean dependency_TMED10 was -0.2816 in the subgroup (n=12577) vs -0.2447 in all other lines; effect=-0.0369, p=2.52e-24.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t test, subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: `feature_010` == `squamous` and `feature_023` in the top quartile (>= 5.669): mean dependency_DHX9 was -0.3985 in the subgroup (n=585) vs -0.3329 in all other lines; effect=-0.0656, p=1.00e-05.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t test, subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016` in the top quartile (>= 0.964): mean dependency_POLR2A was -0.4972 in the subgroup (n=12577) vs -0.3928 in all other lines; effect=-0.1043, p=1.01e-179.

analyses.method: Welch two-sample t test, subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: `feature_018` == 1, `feature_003` in the top quartile (>= 0.428), and `feature_016` in the top quartile (>= 0.964): mean dependency_RPL11 was -0.3966 in the subgroup (n=2483) vs -0.2633 in all other lines; effect=-0.1333, p=9.89e-70.

analysis_notes.dependency_direction: More negative dependency scores indicate stronger knockout dependency.
analysis_notes.continuous_threshold_rule: High and low continuous-feature predicates use empirical top and bottom quartiles.
analysis_notes.systematic_heterogeneity_screen: All dependency outcomes were screened with pairwise predicate interactions over categorical levels, binary features, discrete feature levels, and top/bottom quartiles of continuous features; selected findings are recorded in iteration 8.
